# Stage 2b: Vertical Computational Grid

Defines the vertical level structure for a model component.

**Prerequisites:** None — can be submitted in parallel with Stage 2a.

> [!NOTE]
> Please ensure you are familiar with the completion guidance in the collapsible dropdown where it exists. Failure to do so will result in a failed submission.
> To modify an existing entry, see __[modifying entries](https://github.com/WCRP-CMIP/Essential-Model-Documentation/tree/production?tab=contributing-ov-file#2-modifying-or-reusing-existing-entries)__.

label: Vertical Coordinate
description: The vertical coordinate system used to define level positions.

label: Number of Levels
description: The total number of vertical levels in the grid.

label: Number of Levels (range)
description: The minimum and maximum number of vertical levels where the number varies in time or across the horizontal grid. Only provide if n_z is not set.

label: Top Layer Thickness (m)
description: The thickness of the uppermost layer in metres.

label: Bottom Layer Thickness (m)
description: The thickness of the lowest layer in metres.

label: Total Thickness (m)
description: The total vertical extent of the grid in metres.

label: Additional Collaborators
description: To add attribution to additional members include their GitHub usernames (only) separated by a comma.

label: Additional Information
description: Any additional information that may distinguish this specific grid from others with the same properties.
placeholder: Any additional information that may distinguish this specific grid from others with the same properties. Leave blank if None

## Issue Handling

**After submission:**
- Creates `vertical_computational_grid` (v###)
- Use this ID with a c### in Stage 3 (Model Component)
- [Track progress in issue comments](https://github.com/WCRP-CMIP/Essential-Model-Documentation/issues?q=is%3Aissue%20author%3A%40me)

label: Issue Kind
description: Whether this is a new submission or a modification to an existing entry.
